summary: Bug fix verification completed for Cancel Reservation and Dynamic Availability features. Both bugs are FIXED and working correctly.

minor.issue: Cache invalidation issue - stale data returned without date filter params. Using date filters bypasses cache_warmer and returns correct data.

critical_code_review_comments: Cache invalidation: The bookings endpoint uses cache_warmer which can serve stale data. When cancelling a booking, the cache should be invalidated. However, using date filter params bypasses the cache, so this is a minor issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fixes verified: 1) Cancel reservation flow is working - POST /api/pms-core/cancel properly updates booking status to 'cancelled' and creates a notification with type 'reservation_cancelled'. 2) Rate manager grid now returns dynamic availability with 'sold' count calculated from active bookings. Frontend shows '(X satıldı)' in blue text alongside availability. Note: bookings list endpoint has cache issue - use date filter params to get fresh data.

bug_1_cancel_reservation.description: Cancelled reservation now properly calls backend API
verification: POST /api/pms-core/cancel returns success=true and updates booking status to 'cancelled' in MongoDB
Notification created with type 'reservation_cancelled' containing room number, guest name, dates, and reason
Frontend BookingDetailDialog.js shows Cancel Booking button with data-testid='cancel-booking-btn'
UI displays cancelled bookings with red 'cancelled' badge

bug_2_dynamic_availability.description: Rate manager grid now shows sold count alongside availability
verification: GET /api/channel-manager/rate-manager/grid returns base_availability, sold, and availability fields
Frontend RateManager.jsx displays '(X satıldı)' in blue text when sold > 0
Example: March 18 shows '7 oda (1 satıldı)' - 7 rooms available, 1 sold
